Body: Dear Customer, - Due to recent account takeovers and unauthorized listings, Amazon is requesting a new account verification procedure. >From time to time, randomly selected accounts (seller and/or buyer)are placed under an advanced updating process based on merchant accounts/bank relationsand on-file credit cards. Amazon may also request in an email message scanned/faxed copies of one or more photo ID's. Your account confirmation may go wrong if your credit card/bank account has expired, or if you have changed/replaced your credit card without letting us know about the change. Your account is not suspended, but if in 12 hours after you receive this message your account is not confirmed we reserve the right to terminate your Amazon subscription. If you received this notice and you are not an authorized Amazon account holder, please be aware that it is in violation of Amazon policy to represent oneself as an Amazon user. Such action may also be in violation of local, national, and/or international law. Amazon is committed to assist law enforcement with any inquires related to attempts to misappropriate personal information with the intent to commit fraud or theft. Information will be provided at the request of law enforcement agencies to ensure that perpetrators are prosecuted to the full extent of the law. To confirm your identity with us click the link bellow: We apologize in advance for any inconvenience this may cause you and we would like to thank you for your cooperation as we review this matter. Respectfully,Amazon.com, Inc. Copyright 2006 Amazon.com, Inc. All rights reserved. Amazon sent this e-mail to you because your Notification Preferences indicate that you want to receive information about Special Events & Promotions. Amazon will request personal data (password, credit card/bank numbers) only on our home site, wich is securely incrypted with SLL.

Subject:
[GitHub Support] - A note from GitHub about private information in
 your repository
Body:
## Please do not write below this line ##

----------------------------------------------

GitHub Trust & Safety, Jul 13, 2023, 4:49 PM UTC

Hello,

I'm reaching out on behalf of the GitHub Trust & Safety Team to let you know we received a report that one of your repositories contains private information that was posted without consent. Specifically, the following content was reported:

> https://github.com/rf-peixoto/phishing_pot/blob/master/email/sample_102.eml#L146 (line 146)
https://github.com/rf-peixoto/phishing_pot/blob/master/email/sample_350.eml#L165 (line 165)
https://github.com/rf-peixoto/phishing_pot/blob/master/email/sample_453.eml#L123 (line 123)
https://github.com/rf-peixoto/phishing_pot/blob/master/email/sample_560.eml#L85562 (line 85562)
https://github.com/rf-peixoto/phishing_pot/blob/master/email/sample_560.eml#L88184 (line 88184)

In order to remove the content in question, we ask that you refer to the following article for help:

[Removing sensitive data from a repository](https://docs.github.com/articles/remove-sensitive-data)

***Please make sure to follow those instructions carefully, as simply deleting the content will not remove it completely from the repository commit history.***

Alternately, you may simply want to switch the repository to private by following the instructions found here:

[Making a repository private](https://docs.github.com/en/github/administering-a-repository/setting-repository-visibility#making-a-repository-private)

If these changes are not made within 3 business days, we will continue our review of the complaint. We may need to disable your repository at that time in order to protect the owner of private information that has been posted in violation of our [Acceptable Use Policies](https://docs.github.com/en/github/site-policy/github-acceptable-use-policies#3-conduct-restrictions).

If you have any questions, concerns, or feedback regarding this notice, please let us know as soon as possible.

Regards, 
GitHub Trust & Safety

--------------------------------

[66J721-E1GEV]

Subject: Re: Security Concern: Overlapping Roles in IAM Leading to Potential Data Breach
Body:
Dear Kenneth,

Thank you for contacting Aetheros Support.

We understand you're experiencing issues with overlapping roles in our IAM service. To better assist you, could you please provide us with the following information:

* Specific names of Role A and Role B.
* Examples of resources that are being accessed inappropriately.
* Screenshots of the role configurations for both roles.

Once we have this information, we can investigate the issue and provide a solution.

Best regards,
Aetheros Support Team

Subject: Ola,
Body:
Ola,

Por favor, se você receber esta mensagem em sua pasta SPAM/BULK, devido às
restrições implementadas pelo seu provedor de serviços de Internet, pedimos
que você a trate de forma genuína e gentilmente mova-a para sua caixa de
entrada.

Isto é para notificá-lo de que todos os esforços para contatá-lo foram
malsucedidos. Também enviei vários e-mails, mas nenhuma confirmação. Estou
escrevendo para notificá-lo do relatório de progresso referente aos seus
fundos não reclamados, seguindo as diretrizes das observações iniciais do
Diretor Geral na coletiva de imprensa sobre a COVID-19 na sexta-feira,
Quarta-feira, 11 de junho de 2025.

Fomos instruídos pelas Nações Unidas (ONU)/Banco Mundial e pelo Governo
Federal dos Estados Unidos a liberar todos os fundos/cartões ATM não
reclamados aos beneficiários para reduzir a recessão devido ao surto do
Coronavírus COVID-19.

Todos os nossos esforços para liberar os fundos não reclamados para sua
conta bancária foram infrutíferos. Várias tentativas foram feitas pelo
nosso Departamento de Remessas/Cartões ATM para autorizar o seu cartão
conforme orientação do Governo Federal dos Estados Unidos, mas a
restauração do sistema não conseguiu concluir o processo com êxito. Foi uma
grande surpresa receber esta manhã uma Carta de Autorização dando
autorização à MRS. AMY BARTHEL para reivindicar seus fundos, a carta
identificava a Sra. Amy Barthel como sua família, afirmava que você morreu
meses atrás como resultado do Coronavírus "Covid-19" e você a autorizou a
receber seus fundos de pagamento de Compensação / Herança no valor de US$
10,5 Milhão de dolares.

Por favor, confirme com urgência a autenticidade da Carta de Autorização
enviada por seu parente (Sra. Amy Barthel), pois você pode ver os dados
bancários abaixo, ela já enviou para transferência de fundos. Por favor,
para evitar o envio de seus fundos para uma pessoa errada, atualize-nos com
sua conta bancária atual onde deseja receber os fundos imediatamente. Por
favor, você pode nos fornecer um número pessoal de Telegram ou Telegram
para que possamos entrar em contato com você, ou você pode adicionar e
conversar comigo no WhatsApp +1 (912) 806-8053.

Nome do banco: BANCO DOS EUA DA AMÉRICA
Endereço do banco: 9724 BAY SIDE CT... SPRING HILL FL 34608

Subject: Resolution Center: Your account is limited.

Until we can collect this information, your access to sensitive account features will be limited. We would like to restore your access as soon as possible. We apologize for the inconvenience. Why is my account access limited? Your account access has been limited for the following reason(s): We have reason to believe that your account was accessed by a third party. Because protecting the security of your account is our primary concern, we have limited access to sensitive PayPal account features. We understand that this may be an inconvenience but please understand that this temporary limitation is for your protection. (Your case ID for this reason is PP-192-084-509.) How can I restore my account access? Please visit the Resolution Center and complete the steps to remove limitations. Subject: NedBank Account Statement 18-10-12
Body: Click here to read more Dear Customer Welcome to the movement towards a cleaner, greener, paperless world. Attached to this email is your encrypted electronic statement with enhanced security. To open it simply follow the step-by-step instructions below. Also, please advise us if your email address changes 13 that way we can ensure that you receive your eStatement safely, on time, every time. By opting for eStatements, you 19re making a tangible contribution to shrink your, and Nedbank 19s, carbon footprint. As South Africa 19s green bank, and to show our commitment to a cleaner, greener world, Nedbank will donate 25c to The Green Trust for every eStatement we send. It 19s the small things we do together that add up to make a big difference in curbing climate change. Opening your encrypted statement To open your statement you will need Adobe Reader version 5 or higher. Click here to download. Step 1: Double-click on the attachment. Step 2: Type in your password. Step 3: Click 'OK'. This message has been digitally signed to enable you to verify both its origin and integrity. If the message has been tampered with in any way, a security warning will alert you when you open the mail. To verify the sender 19s digital identity simply click on the red ribbon. Your Password Your password is your account number. To help you avoid confusion if you have multiple current accounts, simply look at the name of the attachment in the email 13 the last four digits are the same as the last four digits in your account number. However, please be sure that you use your full account number when typing in your password(s). Statement Cycle Nedbank will email your statement in accordance with your chosen statement cycle (eg monthly). If we encounter a problem delivering your eStatement 13 eg mailbox is full 13 we will continue trying for up to 24 hours. Thereafter the email will be deemed 18undeliverable 19. If your statements remain undelivered for three consecutive cycles, Nedbank will cease to send you eStatements. To resume the service, simply contact Nedbank on 0860 555 111 and ensure that your eStatements delivery details are up to date. Subject: The crowded race to replace Tory
Body: This Week in Politics What to know about the six leading mayoral candidates ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ THIS WEEK IN POLITICS By David Rider Steve Russell/Toronto Star The race to replace John Tory is now a crowded but focused affair. Crowded because now that registrations are closed, we know there are a record 102 mayoral candidates, their names set in virtual stone. Mail-in ballots look like a small-print page from a big city phone book. Focused because opinion polling from three different firms identify the same leading contenders: ex-police chief Mark Saunders, Coun. Josh Matlow, ex-Liberal MPP Mitzie Hunter, ex-NDP MP Olivia Chow, Coun. Brad Bradford and ex-councillor Ana Bailão. Those polls — showing Chow the frontrunner by a significant margin, usually with Saunders and Matlow next in line — likely reflect those candidates’ high name recognition. Change is likely. Now that we’re in debate season — there were three on Wednesday alone — potential voters should start to tune in and get a sense of the people and their policies, and an idea of what they could do for Toronto’s future rather than just memories of what they’ve done in the past. Debates will continue into June, when we will also see campaign signs sprout across the city like oversized dandelions. Leading campaigns that have been furiously fundraising will unleash a torrent of advertising across multiple mediums to woo voters who can check a box, in advance polls, as early as June 8. Don't miss a thing from your favourite Star columnists. Always get the latest from the voices that matter most to you: pick the columnists you'd like to follow here, and you’ll get an email every time they have a new article online. With one month until most people vote on June 26, here is a subjective look at each of the front-runners, how they are positioning themselves and the challenges they face. Ana Bailão: Council’s former housing advocate inherited much of Tory’s former team. She has raised the most money the quickest and will advertise heavily. She needs a boost in name recognition because polling numbers often place her fourth or fifth. She has positioned herself as a housing expert who can get Premier Doug Ford to upload Toronto’s highways. Ford, however, shot that idea down. With the electorate seemingly in the mood for change, Bailão’s Tory ties might not help her. Brad Bradford: Council’s planning and housing chair is also a former Tory ally fighting to get name recognition and stand out from the pack. He has positioned himself as a “strong mayor of action” poised to blast through bureaucracy to get housing built, transit made safe and more. There’s little evidence it’s working yet, however, and his aggressive debate demeanour could turn off voters. Olivia Chow: Some political observers are surprised at her apparent lead given she hasn’t been in elected politics for eight years. Skeptics note Chow’s early lead in the 2014 mayoral election evaporated. But the vote is not far away and her positioning — an unabashedly progressive leader with a depth of experience — could catch a public mood. Her sometimes fuzzy answers, however, give an opening to rivals’ attacks that will only intensify. Mitzie Hunter: The Scarborough politician is likeable, smart and has rolled out significant policies. She would make history as Toronto’s first Black mayor. Her challenge is to raise her profile and define her progressive message — to “fix the Six” — in a way that vaults her ahead of left-leaning rivals Chow and Matlow, who are better known in civic circles. Josh Matlow: The midtown councillor is running against Ford as much as his mayoral rivals. A past Tory nemesis, he is vowing to abandon Toronto’s “get along to go along” approach to Queen’s Park and fight the interventionist premier to fix the city’s finances, build housing and more. He has lots of thoughtful policy but Chow seems to have pulled ahead as the progressive choice. Matlow’s pointed debate quips ensure he’s quoted, but won’t help dispel talk that he’s not a team player. Mark Saunders: The ex-police chief is trying to portray his lack of political experience as an asset, blaming rivals for all of Toronto’s ills that can be fixed only by his leadership. Saunders is running from the right, hitting hard on voters’ fear of crime and blaming bike lanes for aggravating traffic gridlock. His challenge is to convince voters that close ties to Ford are an asset, and rebut challenges to his record as the city’s top cop. That’s the race as it stands — but, hey, this is Toronto politics. There are 102 reasons it could all change. Here’s more of the Star’s election coverage from my colleagues: Can anyone catch Chow? Here’s what the latest polling shows. Use the Star’s promise tracker to see where the leading candidates stand on key issues. Here’s everything you need to know about how to vote. Columnist Edward Keenan writes about his takeaways and the surprising winner from Wednesday’s debates. Renters could be a force in this election, Victoria Gibson and May Warren report. But will they turn out and vote together? David Rider is city hall bureau chief for the Star. Follow him on Twitter: @dmrider. Subject: Lukka Insights: Lukka’s Role in ETFs, Benchmarks, and Compliance Solutions
Body:
Lukka Newsletter

Hi phishing@pot!

In this issue of the Lukka newsletter, we present the latest updates from both content and product perspectives, highlighting how Lukka continues to deliver precision and reliability in the digital asset space.

Content : Discover how Lukka’s accurate crypto asset data supports the rise of crypto Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs), offering critical insights into this innovative investment opportunity. Additionally, learn how Lukka’s data solutions are being integrated to enhance analytics and AML compliance across the digital asset industry.
Product : Explore Lukka Crypto Benchmarks, the industry standard for digital asset benchmarking, and learn how it provides reliable solutions for ETFs, derivatives, and AML compliance.

Don’t miss out on these updates—read on to learn more!

LUKKA EXPERTISE

Crypto ETFs: How Lukka Ensures Precision in a Complex Market

The investment world witnessed a paradigm shift with the emergence of crypto Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs). Crypto ETFs combine traditional ETF structures with the rapidly evolving crypto market, creating new opportunities for investors.

Explore how Crypto ETFs have risen to prominence and the pivotal role Lukka's real-time, accurate crypto asset data plays in this new investment era.

Read more >>

PRODUCT HIGHLIGHT

Lukka Crypto Benchmarks Trusted Solutions for Digital Asset Benchmarking

Lukka Crypto Benchmarks offers the most reliable and flexible digital asset benchmark services, adhering to IOSCO Principles for Financial Benchmarks. Designed to meet the needs of exchange-traded funds (ETFs), derivative contracts, and other financial products, Lukka Benchmarks sets the standard for accuracy and integrity in the rapidly evolving digital asset market.

Lukka Benchmarks offers a comprehensive suite of solutions for digital asset financial products:

Proprietary and Redistributed Data : Tailored to meet diverse use cases with custom data solutions.

Pre-Established and Custom Benchmarks : For enhanced flexibility in tracking market performance.

FMV Data for NAV Post-Trade Reporting : Delivering accurate valuations for financial reporting needs.

Calculation Agent Services : Supporting financial products with robust benchmark calculations.

AML Compliance and Risk Reporting : Ensuring transparency and security for tokenized assets.

Learn more about Lukka Benchmarks and discover how we can support your digital asset benchmarking needs.

Contact us today

LATEST NEWS

Amber Group Integrates Lukka Insights

Amber Group, a leading global digital asset company, announced a strategic integration of Lukka Insights into Amber Group’s data.

Read more >>

Matter Labs Utilizes Lukka’s Data Solutions

Matter Labs, a contributor to Ethereum-based ZKsync, will use Lukka’s leading blockchain data solution for analytics and AML compliance.

Read more >>

About Lukka

Founded in 2014, Lukka serves the most risk-mature businesses in the world with institutional data and software solutions. As a global company, headquartered in the United States, Lukka bridges the gap between the complexities of blockchain data in a global crypto ecosystem with traditional business and reporting requirements.

All of Lukka’s products are created with institutional standards, such as AICPA Service and Organization Controls (SOC), which focus on data quality, financial calculation accuracy & completeness, and managing technology operational risk. Lukka has obtained AICPA SOC 1 Type II and SOC 2 Type II Audits, an ISO/IEC-27001 certification, NIST Cybersecurity Assessment, and continues to lead the industry with best in class technology risk governance.

Our global team looks forward to partnering with you to solve your data challenges.

lukka.tech

Subject: Trump heads to court; May inflation numbers; Reddit "goes dark"
Body: Plus, these carpenters are rebuilding the Notre Dame Cathedral using techniques from the middle ages. View this email online by Suzanne Nuyen June 13, 2023 Good morning. The Denver Nuggets beat the Miami Heat last night to earn its first NBA Finals title in the team's 47-year history. Here's what else we're following today. Former President Donald Trump is expected to make his first appearance in Miami court this afternoon to face federal criminal charges in a 37-count indictment. His aide, Walt Nauta, has also been indicted for concealing documents and making false statements. Here's what to expect today. Win McNamee/Getty Images 🎧 NPR's Carrie Johnson reports on Up First this morning that special counsel Jack Smith wants a speedy trial, so it's possible it could end well before the presidential primary season, but the defense has the power to delay the trial. 🎧 Trump's supporters have questioned why similar cases involving classified documents haven't gotten the same attention. On Morning Edition, former federal prosecutor Robert Mintz says the indictment is about what Trump did with the documents after the government requested their return, not what documents he had. The Labor Department will release its latest inflation numbers this morning while the Federal Reserve decides whether it will raise interest rates again. 🎧 NPR's Scott Horsley says grocery prices have been coming down in recent months, and gas prices are holding steady. But the price of services like restaurant meals and car repairs is still climbing. Horsley adds Fed watchers predict the central bank will leave interest rates alone this month, but a Vanguard economist thinks it could resume raising rates in July. Artificial intelligence is likely to transform many industries in the future. But in the surveillance industry, the revolution is already underway. 🎧 "AI is really good at pattern recognition," says NPR's Geoff Brumfiel. He reports privacy and civil rights experts are worried about how technology like facial recognition can be used by law enforcement to track citizens. Brumfiel adds countries like China, Israel and Singapore have already set up surveillance systems, and the issue is "something that global society really needs to start looking at." Thousands of Reddit communities have "gone dark" in protest of new fees the company will start charging third-party developers. 🎧 The pricing is so high that many third-party apps will have to shut down, according to NPR's Bobby Allyn. The new focus on money comes as Reddit sets its sights on going public. The communities hope that by "going dark," they can get the company to negotiate. Newsletter continues after sponsor message Cristina Baussan for NPRYou won't hear any electric saws or see any nails and screws as carpenters from France's Ateliers Perrault work to rebuild framework that supports the roof of Notre Dame Cathedral. Photos show workers at the 250-year-old company working by hand to rebuild the cathedral by the end of 2024 in its traditional way from the middle ages. 🎧 Listen to carpenters explain the importance of preserving this art form. Giorgia Viera/AFP via Getty Images— Steve Inskeep, Morning Edition hostIt’s common to lament that in divided America, people do not agree on the same set of facts. This is not quite true with Donald Trump’s indictment. Nobody questions that Trump took numerous documents when he left office in 2021. Nobody denies that he failed to return them when asked. Photos from his indictment make it plain how he stored them: crates of papers were left in a bathroom or on a ballroom stage. The sole dispute is whether the former president had some authority to use the documents or some latitude in breaking the rules. His supporters have loudly claimed that he did; but lately they have more often changed the subject — suggesting without evidence that the Justice Department was “weaponized” against their leader or that a “double standard” applied because Hillary Clinton was not prosecuted in a different case in 2016. In a way, the “double standard” argument is an affirmation of the underlying fact at the center of the case: Trump had classified documents in his home. Quintana Beach County Park/Anadolu Agency via Getty Imagesadded Thousands of dead fish washed up on the shores of beaches in southeast Texas this weekend. The state's Parks and Wildlife Department says the fish suffocated due to low oxygen in the water.Elizabeth Gilbert, the author of Eat, Pray, Love, says she's delaying the release of her new book after backlash from Ukrainians about its setting in Russia.Andy Davis was diagnosed with heart failure a few days before he and his wife planned to bike across the country. He honors his heart donor and unsung hero, Sarah Ivy. Listen to your local NPR station. Visit NPR.org to hear live radio from KUNC (edit station). This newsletter was edited by Majd Al-Waheidi. Enjoying this newsletter? Forward to a friend! They can sign up here.Looking for more great content? Check out all of our newsletter offerings — including Music, Politics, Health and more!
